evaluations.query: I need to configure Vite to build for three targets: browser client, Node.js SSR server, and Cloudflare edge workers. How do I set up multi-environment builds?
expected_behavior: Claude uses the Vite 7 Environment API with the environments config key
Configures separate client, ssr, and edge environment blocks
Sets appropriate build targets (e.g., node20 for SSR, edge/worker conditions for edge)
Uses separate outDir for each environment (dist/client, dist/server, dist/edge)
Mentions that each environment has its own module graph

evaluations.query: Our Vite 7 build takes 46 seconds and we're considering Vite 8 with Rolldown. Show me how to migrate manualChunks to the new advancedChunks API.
expected_behavior: Claude explains the migration from manualChunks to advancedChunks with declarative groups
Provides advancedChunks config with priority, minSize, maxSize, and minShareCount fields
Mentions the rolldown-vite gradual migration path as an option
References performance benchmarks (7.7x build speed improvement)
Includes the migration checklist items for verifying a complete upgrade

evaluations.query: Write a React component that displays a data table with sorting and filtering
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the vite-advanced skill
Implements a standard React data table component
Uses UI component patterns without build configuration concerns

evaluations.query: Split our Vite 8 build into separate vendor chunks for React, router, and remaining dependencies with size limits.
expected_behavior: Uses advancedChunks groups with regex test patterns to match node_modules packages
Assigns priority values to resolve conflicts between overlapping chunk groups
Sets maxSize on the catch-all vendor group to auto-split oversized bundles
Creates a dedicated react-vendor chunk for react, react-dom, and scheduler at highest priority
Recommends minShareCount for shared UI libraries imported by multiple routes

evaluations.query: Configure separate Vite environments for client browser, Node.js SSR, and Cloudflare edge worker builds.
expected_behavior: Uses the environments config key with separate client, ssr, and edge blocks
Puts only shared settings at the top level and environment-specific settings under each block
Sets separate outDir for each environment to prevent build output conflicts
Configures resolve.noExternal true and edge conditions for the edge runtime environment
References this.environment in plugin hooks for environment-aware transformations

evaluations.query: Configure Vite to build a React component library with proper externals, dual ESM/CJS output, and TypeScript declarations.
expected_behavior: Externalizes peer dependencies like react and react-dom via rollupOptions.external
Provides dual format output with ESM for bundlers and UMD or CJS for legacy consumers
Uses vite-plugin-dts to generate TypeScript declaration files alongside build output
Configures package.json exports map with import, require, and types subpath entries
Marks CSS files in sideEffects to preserve styles during consumer tree-shaking

evaluations.query: Create a Vite plugin with a virtual module that injects build-time data and only runs during production builds.
expected_behavior: Uses resolveId and load hooks together for virtual module creation instead of transform
Prefixes resolved virtual module IDs with the null byte character to exclude from filesystem
Sets apply to build to restrict the plugin to vite build mode only
Uses enforce pre or post to control plugin execution order relative to core plugins
Returns valid JavaScript module source from the load hook with JSON serialized data

evaluations.query: My Vite environment config has the same plugin registered at both the top level and inside the ssr environment block, and the build produces duplicate transforms
expected_behavior: Explains that plugins at the top level apply to all environments and duplicating them in a specific environment causes double processing
Recommends placing shared plugins at the top level only and environment-specific plugins only in their block
Does not suggest removing the plugin from the top level without checking if other environments need it
Advises using the apply function or environment checks within plugin hooks to conditionally run transforms

evaluations.query: My advancedChunks config references a package in the test regex that isn't installed and the build silently produces a 2MB vendor chunk instead of splitting
expected_behavior: Explains that regex patterns matching no modules result in empty groups and the catch-all absorbs everything
Recommends verifying that the package names in test patterns match actual node_modules directory names
Does not suggest increasing maxSize as the fix for a misconfigured chunk group regex
Advises using the Rolldown build analyzer or stats output to verify which modules land in which chunks

evaluations.query: I created a Vite virtual module that injects process.env into the client bundle for runtime config access
expected_behavior: Warns that injecting the full process.env object exposes all server environment variables to the client bundle
Recommends explicitly allowlisting only the specific VITE_ prefixed variables needed on the client side
Does not validate injecting DATABASE_URL or API_SECRET into client-side code
Advises using import.meta.env which Vite already filters to only VITE_ prefixed variables
